title: Crunchy Herbal Tofu Stir-fry
description: A show-off Croatian dish featuring marinated tofu, infused with herbs and served as a vegetarian-friendly meal.

ingredients.name: Paprika powder
ingredients.notes: To add a touch of heat and color

ingredients.name: Water (for soaking tofu)
ingredients.notes: Necessary to soften the tofu before use

ingredients.name: Bok Choy stalks
ingredients.notes: For a green pop and extra crunch

instructions: Soak the tofu in water for about 30 minutes. Drain and cube it.
Marinate the cubed tofu with olive oil, paprika powder, salt (to taste), and any other desired herbs or spices like thyme, basil, or dill.
Preheat a wok or large skillet over high heat. Add olive oil to coat the bottom of the pan.
Once hot, add shallot slices to the pan. Cook until they start to brown and release their flavors (about 2-3 minutes).
Add minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Gently fold in the marinated tofu cubes, allowing them to sit and absorb some of the oil and flavor from the shallots and garlic.
Season with salt and pepper based on your preference. Stir well to combine all ingredients evenly distributed.
Once cooked through, remove tofu from the pan and set aside.
In the same pan, add chopped bok choy if using. Cook until just wilted (about 2-3 minutes).
Return the tofu cubes back into the pan with the bok choy. Toss everything together ensuring a well-coordinated stir-fry process to meld all flavors.
Serve hot and garnish with fresh watercress leaves if desired for additional crunchiness.
